title: Connecting sub items to items across boards
excerpt: Hi. Building out my first implementation of Monday.com, so exciting times here, but hitting a few challenges along the way (nothing new, I’ve been native in Workfront for years and that’s really refines your tolerance fo…

posts.author: rt-ops (Andy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi. Building out my first implementation of Monday.com, so exciting times here, but hitting a few challenges along the way (nothing new, I’ve been native in Workfront for years and that’s really refines your tolerance for challenges).

I have a board where my team tasks are managed, with each project represented by an item and each task as a sub-item - because of the nature of our work, this is the only way it can be structured).
When a subitem/task is ready for quality checking, I have an automation that creates a new corresponding item on a QA Board, but I ideally want to link that newly-created item to the originating sub-item, such that when the item on the QA board moves to QA complete as its status, this updates the parent subitem status to QA Complete for its status.
Is there a way as part of automation or workflow to connect them in this way?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

posts.author: rt-ops (Andy)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for the response - I think I’ve found a way to resolve my issue with a bit of trial and error and perseverance. But just to respond to your actual question, I have Work Management, Enterprise, in case there’s a particularly great solution you could suggest. Thank you!

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey Andy! Shannon here with the monday team. Happy to hear that you’ve found a way to resolve this. You go!! If you need any additional eyes as you update or modify the solution, let us know and we can brainstorm together.
